name: Summarize Google Sheets form feedback via OpenAI's GPT-4
description: Automated workflow: Summarize Google Sheets form feedback via OpenAI's GPT-4. This workflow integrates 8 different services: stickyNote, markdown, stopAndError, manualTrigger, gmail. It contains 12 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: Summarize Google Sheets form feedback via OpenAI's GPT-4 workflow containing 11 nodes.

originalWorkflowJson.name: Summarize Google Sheets form feedback via OpenAI's GPT-4

nodes.name: When clicking "Test workflow"
nodes.notes: This manualTrigger node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
### 1. Create a Google Sheet document
* This tutorial uses Google Sheet document connected to Google Forms, but a standalone Sheet document will work too
* Adapt initial trigger to your needs: run manually or at some time intervals

[Link to the Google Sheets template]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
### 2. Combine all answers into an array
* Since the main goal is to provide an overall summary, we need to combine all answers for each Google Form question
* Aggregate Node takes multiple incoming items and produces just a single item which contains arrays of user feedback

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
### 3. Generate a summary report
* Enter a __system message__ with a overall instructions on how to analyze the feedback form
* Provide a __user message__ with JSON arrays.

__NB! Consider splitting the form questions for a very long forms or when the number of responses is too high__

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
### 4. Convert to HTML and send an email
* GPT is configured to reply in Markdown format. Markdown Node converts such text into HTML
* Finally, the Gmail node sends a message with HTML report

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Get Google Sheets records
nodes.notes: This googleSheets node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Aggregate responses into arrays

fieldToAggregate.fieldToAggregate: ['What is the chance of recommending our event?']

nodes.notes: This aggregate node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Summarize via GPT model

messages.content:
Your task is to summarize event feedback form responses. You will receive answers on three questions:
1. What went great?
2. How can we improve?
3. What is the chance of recommending our event?

Each questions has several answers separated by | character.
Analyze each question and prepare a summary report. It should contain an overall sentiment regarding the event, followed by the constructive ideas of what to improve.

Reply in Markdown formatting

=1. What went great: ```{{ $json['What went great?'].join(' | ') }}```
2. How can we improve: ```{{ $json['How can we improve?'].join(' | ') }}```
3. What is the chance of recommending our event: ```{{ $json['What is the chance of recommending our event?'].join(' | ') }}```

nodes.notes: This openAi node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Convet from Markdown to HTML
nodes.notes: This markdown node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Send via Gmail
nodes.notes: This gmail node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

fieldToAggregate.fieldToAggregate: ['What is the chance of recommending our event?']

parameters.content:
### 1. Create a Google Sheet document
* This tutorial uses Google Sheet document connected to Google Forms, but a standalone Sheet document will work too
* Adapt initial trigger to your needs: run manually or at some time intervals

[Link to the Google Sheets template]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

### 2. Combine all answers into an array
* Since the main goal is to provide an overall summary, we need to combine all answers for each Google Form question
* Aggregate Node takes multiple incoming items and produces just a single item which contains arrays of user feedback

### 3. Generate a summary report
* Enter a __system message__ with a overall instructions on how to analyze the feedback form
* Provide a __user message__ with JSON arrays.

__NB! Consider splitting the form questions for a very long forms or when the number of responses is too high__

### 4. Convert to HTML and send an email
* GPT is configured to reply in Markdown format. Markdown Node converts such text into HTML
* Finally, the Gmail node sends a message with HTML report

messages.content:
Your task is to summarize event feedback form responses. You will receive answers on three questions:
1. What went great?
2. How can we improve?
3. What is the chance of recommending our event?

Each questions has several answers separated by | character.
Analyze each question and prepare a summary report. It should contain an overall sentiment regarding the event, followed by the constructive ideas of what to improve.

Reply in Markdown formatting

=1. What went great: ```{{ $json['What went great?'].join(' | ') }}```
2. How can we improve: ```{{ $json['How can we improve?'].join(' | ') }}```
3. What is the chance of recommending our event: ```{{ $json['What is the chance of recommending our event?'].join(' | ') }}```

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking "Test workflow"".
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Google Sheets records" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Aggregate responses into arrays" node (n8n-nodes-base.aggregate) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the AI node "Summarize via GPT model".
Adjust the Model settings (e.g. gpt-4o, gpt-3.5-turbo) and write or refine the System Prompt parameters.
Open the "Convet from Markdown to HTML" node (n8n-nodes-base.markdown) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send via Gmail" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Incoming triggers will qualified/formatted, new rows will be written to Google Sheets, and email replies or alerts will be sent automatically.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
